paper_title: Volatile compounds and palynological analysis from pollen pots of stingless bees from the mid-north region of Brazil

methods.description: This method describes the process of identifying volatile constituents in samples using Gas Chromatography coupled with Mass Spectrometry (GC-MS) after methylation with diazomethane. The method is used to analyze the chemical composition of volatile compounds in pollen pots of stingless bees.

steps.description: After methylation with diazomethane, the identification of volatile constituents was made by GC-MS.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform methylation with diazomethane and identify volatile constituents using GC-MS

steps.description: Use a Shimadzu mass spectrometer model QP5050A equipped with a DB-5 HT column for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up Shimadzu mass spectrometer with DB-5 HT column for analysis

steps.description: Set temperature: injector at 220°C, detector at 240°C, column 60°C to 240°C with heating rate of 3°C/min.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure temperature settings for GC-MS analysis

steps.description: Select helium as the carrier gas with a flow rate of 1 mL/min.
steps.searchable_summary: Set helium as carrier gas with specified flow rate

steps.description: Acquire mass spectrum in the range 40-650 Daltons by electron impact ionization with ionization energy of 70 eV and ion source at 200°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire mass spectrum using electron impact ionization

steps.description: Compare mass spectra to entries from the electronic library Wiley229 for identification.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify compounds by comparing mass spectra to Wiley229 library

steps.description: Calculate Kovats indices based on retention times of hydrocarbon standards (C10-C25) injected under the same conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Kovats indices using retention times of hydrocarbon standards
Validate results by performing duplicate analyses
